claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training? (For example, is he studying at school, college, or undertaking an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and isn't studying anywhere right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. One more important question:

Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at the moment. He's just taking some time to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one final question to complete my assessment:

Has Alex registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services? (If a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with either of these, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for up to 20 weeks.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't registered with any careers service or the armed forces.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can continue to receive Child Benefit only if they are under 20 **and** staying in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in approved education or training, he doesn't meet the criteria for continued Child Benefit eligibility.

**However**, if Alex decides to return to education or training in the future, you may become eligible again as long as he does so before turning 20. Additionally, if he were to register with a government-sponsored careers service while figuring out his next steps, that could potentially help, though the 20-week extension for registering with careers services specifically applies to 16-17 year olds who have just left education.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your circumstances?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll still qualify if they are under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. There is an exception that allows Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. However, Alex is 18 (not 16 or 17), is not in approved education or training, has not registered with a careers service or armed services, and is not working. Since Alex does not meet any of the criteria for continued Child Benefit eligibility after age 16, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and lives with them. While the claimant is responsible for Alex, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit as he is over 16 and not in approved education or training. He also hasn't registered with careers services that would allow a temporary extension of benefits.

He also hasn't registered with careers services that would allow a temporary extension of benefits.
